profile.name: Janiyah Tan
profile.tagline: Designing at the intersection of circuits and screens.
profile.bio: I'm an upcoming second-year IT student at Lorma Colleges with a soft spot for anything that blinks, clicks, or lights up. I love taking apart hardware just to understand how it thinks, and I bring that same curiosity into digital design — sketching interfaces the way I'd wire a breadboard: piece by piece, until every connection makes sense. For me, good UI/UX isn't just how something looks, it's how it works — clean, intentional, and built to be understood.

projects.title: CircuitCart
projects.description: A UI concept for a hardware marketplace app where hobbyists can browse components. Focused on clear category iconography and a mobile-first checkout flow.

projects.title: Breadboard Portfolio
projects.description: This very site — a JSON-driven portfolio built to practice separating content from layout, styled around a circuit-board visual language.

projects.title: Signal — Habit Tracker Wireframes
projects.description: Low to high-fidelity wireframes for a habit-tracking app, exploring how small hardware-style indicators (LED-like status dots) can simplify daily check-ins.

education.title: BS Information Technology
education.description: Currently a 2nd year student, building a foundation in web development, design fundamentals, and systems thinking.

education.title: Senior High School, STEM/ICT Strand
education.description: Developed an early interest in electronics and hardware tinkering alongside foundational computing coursework.
